skill.name: type-safety-validation
skill.description: Build end-to-end type-safe TypeScript applications with Zod runtime validation, tRPC APIs, Prisma ORM, and modern TypeScript patterns.
skill.summary: Guide TypeScript teams through typed validation, APIs, database access, and full-stack examples.
skill.author: AI Agent Hub

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many high-risk patterns, but review shows they are markdown code fences, TypeScript examples, documentation URLs, and relative imports. No malicious behavior or prompt injection was found. One low-risk documentation issue remains because a sample tRPC mutation uses publicProcedure without showing authentication.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Detections Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to markdown code fences and TypeScript sample blocks, not runnable Ruby or shell backtick execution. The skill contains instructional examples, and no executable script file or command invocation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are markdown fence boundaries for documentation samples. I found no executable file or instruction that runs these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: Network And Filesystem Detections Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are localhost, example API, and public documentation links. The filesystem findings are TypeScript method chaining and a relative import inside sample code, not hidden file access or path traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows tutorial code and resource links only. There is no data exfiltration endpoint, downloader, or runtime filesystem operation.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Detections Are Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The blocker findings map to general TypeScript, Prisma, or decorator terminology such as descriptions, descending sort order, and type utilities. No hashing algorithm, host discovery, scanning logic, or reconnaissance behavior was present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible documentation and ordinary TypeScript syntax. Their semantic context does not match cryptography or reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Sample Mutation Omits Authentication Context
low_findings.description: The full-stack example uses publicProcedure for a create mutation and does not show authorization checks. This is not malicious, but users could copy the sample into a production API without adding access control.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sample clearly creates data through a public procedure. The surrounding document is a type-safety guide, so the omission is a documentation caveat rather than confirmed vulnerable deployed code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unauthenticated Write Procedure In Sample Code
dangerous_patterns.description: The create mutation is demonstrated with publicProcedure and no authorization layer. Marketplace users should treat it as a teaching sample and add authentication before using it in production.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code sample has a public write path, but it is documentation rather than installed application code. Risk depends on whether users copy it without changes.

content.value_statement: TypeScript teams often lose type safety between validation, APIs, database models, and UI code. This skill shows how to connect Zod, tRPC, Prisma, and TypeScript patterns into one typed workflow.
actual_capabilities: Explains Zod schemas, inferred types, refinements, transforms, and discriminated unions.
Shows tRPC server and client patterns for typed API procedures.
Demonstrates Prisma schema and query examples with typed results.
Connects backend and frontend examples in a Next.js-style tRPC workflow.
Lists practical TypeScript configuration and validation best practices.
limitations: It is a documentation-only skill and does not run validation tools automatically.
Examples need adaptation for project-specific authentication and authorization rules.
It does not cover every TypeScript framework or database provider.
Some version references may need confirmation against current package releases.

use_cases.title: Design a Typed API
use_cases.description: Plan request validation, response types, and client usage before implementing a new TypeScript API.

use_cases.title: Improve Form And Input Validation
use_cases.description: Convert informal validation rules into reusable Zod schemas with clear error handling.

use_cases.title: Audit Full-Stack Type Boundaries
use_cases.description: Review where database, API, and UI types diverge, then create a safer shared contract.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Zod Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define a Zod schema for this user input shape. Include inferred TypeScript types and safe error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Design a tRPC Procedure
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a tRPC procedure for this feature. Include input validation, output shape, and client usage guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Connect Prisma and Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Prisma model and suggest matching validation schemas for create, update, and query operations.

prompt_templates.title: Audit End-to-End Type Safety
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this TypeScript feature for type-safety gaps across UI, API, validation, and database layers. Prioritize fixes by risk.

output_examples.input: A product team needs safer user registration input handling.
output: A registration schema with email, password, role, and optional profile fields.
A typed result model for successful validation and structured validation errors.
Guidance to keep password policy, normalization, and user-facing messages consistent.

output_examples.input: An engineering team wants a typed posts API.
output: A recommended tRPC procedure structure for list and create operations.
Input boundaries for pagination, titles, and optional content.
A reminder to add authentication to write procedures before production use.

output_examples.input: A TypeScript codebase has duplicated API and database types.
output: A review of redundant type definitions and likely drift points.
A plan to derive types from schemas or router definitions.
Migration steps that reduce manual casting and unsafe any usage.

best_practices: Validate data at every external boundary, including forms, APIs, webhooks, and environment variables.
Derive TypeScript types from validation schemas or API routers instead of duplicating contracts.
Add authentication and authorization checks around write operations before using examples in production.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on compile-time types for untrusted runtime input.
Do not copy public write procedure examples into production without access control.
Do not silence type errors with any when a schema or type guard can model the data.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and examples for type-safe TypeScript development.

faq.question: Which libraries does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Zod, tRPC, Prisma, and modern TypeScript patterns.

faq.question: Can it replace a security review?
faq.answer: No. It improves type and validation design, but security controls still need separate review.

faq.question: Is it useful for existing projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help identify unsafe boundaries and plan a gradual migration to stronger types.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users check before production use?
faq.answer: Users should verify package versions, add authorization, and adapt examples to their application architecture.
